story.problem: JavaScript's standard library is famously sparse. There's no built-in groupBy, chunk, debounce, cloneDeep, or flatten. Every project either wrote these utilities from scratch or copy-pasted snippets. Lodash became the de facto standard library for JavaScript by providing over 200 well-tested utility functions.
story.mental_model: Think of lodash as the standard library JavaScript should have had. It follows functional programming conventions: functions take data as their first argument, are composable, and never mutate inputs. The _(collection).chain().map().filter().value() syntax lets you build data transformation pipelines clearly.
story.when_to_use: Use lodash for array manipulation (chunk, flatten, groupBy, uniq), object utilities (cloneDeep, merge, pick, omit), function helpers (debounce, throttle, memoize), and collection operations. Import only the functions you need (import groupBy from 'lodash/groupBy') to keep bundle size down.
story.when_not_to_use: Modern JavaScript (ES2020+) has absorbed many lodash features natively: Array.flat(), Object.fromEntries(), optional chaining, nullish coalescing. Audit what you actually need before adding lodash — you may already have it in the language.

alternatives.name: radash
alternatives.reason: Modern lodash alternative written in TypeScript with better tree-shaking and async utilities

alternatives.name: remeda
alternatives.reason: Type-safe utility library with data-first and data-last variants for better inference

alternatives.name: es-toolkit
alternatives.reason: Drop-in lodash replacement with smaller bundle size and modern TypeScript types

nodes.label: lodash
nodes.summary: Import as `import _ from 'lodash'` or cherry-pick: `import chunk from 'lodash/chunk'`.

nodes.label: Array Utilities
nodes.summary: chunk, compact, difference, flatten, flattenDeep, intersection, union, uniq, zip, take, drop.

nodes.label: Collection Methods
nodes.summary: filter, find, findIndex, map, reduce, forEach, groupBy, sortBy, orderBy, countBy, keyBy.

nodes.label: Object Utilities
nodes.summary: assign, merge, cloneDeep, pick, omit, has, get, set, keys, values, entries, mapKeys, mapValues.

nodes.label: String Utilities
nodes.summary: camelCase, kebabCase, snakeCase, capitalize, trim, truncate, escape, pad, repeat, template.

nodes.label: Function Utilities
nodes.summary: debounce, throttle, memoize, once, partial, curry, bind, flip, negate, wrap.

nodes.label: Math Utilities
nodes.summary: add, subtract, multiply, divide, ceil, floor, round, clamp, inRange, random, sum, sumBy, mean.

nodes.label: Lang / Type Checks
nodes.summary: isArray, isObject, isString, isNumber, isNil, isEqual, isEmpty, cloneDeep, toArray, toNumber.

nodes.label: _.chain() / Lazy Evaluation
nodes.summary: Wrap a value in a chain to compose operations. Call .value() to execute.
